novel_features.name: Front-page diff
novel_features.description: See exactly what climbed, fell, appeared, or dropped off the front page since your last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs require two list snapshots side by side; the live API cannot tell you what just changed because there is no rank-over-time endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent wakes up daily and needs to know what shifted on HN since yesterday — without re-fetching 500 items every poll.

novel_features.name: Topic pulse
novel_features.description: See per-day mentions, average score, and comment volume for any topic over the last N days.
novel_features.rationale: Algolia returns hits, never aggregations. Bucketing across day windows requires fan-out plus local reduction.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the question is 'is this topic heating up or cooling down' rather than 'what's the top story right now'.

novel_features.name: Hiring thread aggregator
novel_features.description: Aggregate the last N monthly Who-is-Hiring threads: top languages, remote ratio, top companies, location distribution.
novel_features.rationale: Single-month filtering already exists in haxor-news; what is impossible without a local store is comparing across months. Fetching whoishiring's submitted IDs plus tokenizing posts produces the cross-month view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need quarterly or seasonal hiring trends — language popularity, remote-share shifts, location density — not just this month's listings.

novel_features.name: Hiring company tracker
novel_features.description: Companies that posted in M of the last N hiring threads, with first-seen, last-seen, and months-posted count.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-month joins on a normalized company token are impossible from a single API call. The local store keeps the canonical text; mechanical normalization handles capitalization and punctuation drift.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when sourcing or trend-tracking — which companies are persistent hirers vs one-off posters — without scraping HNHIRING.com.

novel_features.name: Controversial
novel_features.description: Stories ranked by the highest comment-to-point ratio over a recent window — the discussions everyone is arguing about.
novel_features.rationale: No Algolia or Firebase endpoint surfaces this metric. It is one SQL line over the synced items table, but only with the items table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you want stories with high engagement-to-approval — heated debate signal — instead of just popularity.

novel_features.name: Repost finder
novel_features.description: Has this URL been posted before? Lists every prior submission, with score, comments, and date.
novel_features.rationale: Algolia search restricted to the URL attribute; rank by points; one round-trip — but the convenience matters when the alternative is opening a browser tab and pasting.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before submitting a Show HN — duplicate URLs flame out instantly; you want to know how a prior post did first.

novel_features.name: Story velocity
novel_features.description: Show a story's rank trajectory over time from local snapshots — climb, plateau, or fall.
novel_features.rationale: Rank-over-time has no API surface. Sync's snapshot history is the only way to reconstruct the curve.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent asks 'is this story still gaining traction or already cresting' — only meaningful answer comes from snapshots.

novel_features.name: User stats
novel_features.description: Median and p90 score across a user's submissions, plus traction buckets and hour-of-day score distribution.
novel_features.rationale: HN's profile page lists submitted IDs but no traction summary. Joining users.submitted with synced items computes the view from primitives the API already returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before posting your own work to learn your traction patterns, or when sizing up a poster's history before engaging.

novel_features.name: Local FTS search
novel_features.description: Offline full-text search over every story and comment you have ever synced — corpus grows with use.
novel_features.rationale: Algolia drops/reindexes history and is online-only. SQLite FTS5 over title+text gives you a personal corpus that compounds.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when investigating long-tail topics or replaying last quarter's research — Algolia might rank it down or drop it; your local corpus will not.

novel_features.name: Sync foundation
novel_features.description: Pull top/new/best/show/ask/job lists and recently-changed items into local SQLite for offline use and snapshot history.
novel_features.rationale: Every other novel command reads from the store this populates. Firebase /maxitem + /updates supports incremental sync without re-fetching.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this once per day (or per hour for agents) — it is the foundation that turns since/velocity/controversial/users-stats from impossible into one SQL query.

narrative.headline: Hacker News from your terminal — with a local SQLite store, snapshot history, and agent-native output no other HN tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Combines the Firebase real-time API and the Algolia search API in one CLI. Sync once and run cross-month hiring digests, topic pulses, repost lookups, and rank-over-time queries against a local store — offline, scriptable, MCP-ready. Every command supports --json and --select; the API itself is read-only, so this CLI is too.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication needed — both the Firebase and Algolia HN APIs are free and public.

quickstart.comment: Pull current top/new/best/show/ask/job lists plus recent items into the local store
Track a topic's per-day mentions, score, and comment volume
See exactly what changed on the front page since the last sync
Aggregate the last 3 months of Who-is-Hiring — languages, remote ratio, top companies

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm both APIs respond: hackernews-pp-cli doctor
Algolia indexes lag a few minutes; use stories top for the freshest list

troubleshoots.symptom: items thread <id> times out on huge threads
troubleshoots.fix: Use --depth 2 to cap tree depth, or --flat for a linear view
Run sync at least twice with a delay; since needs at least two snapshots in the lists table

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for hackernews-pp-cli when you need to monitor or analyze HN signal programmatically: agent-driven daily diffs, topic pulses, hiring-thread aggregation, repost checks before submitting, structured thread digests for context windows. The local store makes follow-up queries cheap and offline-friendly.

recipes.title: Daily front-page diff for an agent
recipes.explanation: Sync, then diff. The --select narrows the payload to just the change deltas — agents process kilobytes instead of megabytes.

recipes.title: Topic monitoring with selected fields
recipes.explanation: Per-day breakdown of mentions, average score, and comment volume — using --select to pull only the trend axes from the deeply-nested response.

recipes.title: Pre-submit dupe check
recipes.explanation: Lists every prior submission of that URL with score, comments, and date — answers the 'has this been posted' question in one round-trip.

recipes.title: Filter Who's Hiring for remote Go roles
recipes.explanation: Regex against the latest monthly thread. The double-escaped \\b is a real regex word boundary; --json gives you structured rows instead of one big match dump.

recipes.title: Quarterly hiring trend
recipes.explanation: Companies posting in 3+ of the last 6 monthly threads — first-seen, last-seen, total months. Cross-month queries you cannot do without a local store.
